attributed to Vulca

1 work in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Apollo of Veii
by attributed to Vulca
Etruscan · c. 510 BCE · Veii
A full-size polychrome terracotta from the roofline of the Portonaccio sanctuary, striding forward to contest Herakles for a stolen deer. Its broad smile and swinging drapery put Etruscan sculpture at parity with anything being made in archaic Greece.
←Paolo Veronese Antoine Watteau→
